cities.name: Chandigarh
cities.tagline: The City Beautiful — India's best-planned city
cities.overview: Chandigarh, the joint capital of Punjab and Haryana, is India's first planned city designed by Swiss-French architect Le Corbusier. Known for its clean streets, beautiful gardens, and high standard of living.
cities.history: Built in the 1950s by Le Corbusier, Chandigarh replaced Lahore as the capital of Punjab after Partition. It is a masterpiece of modernist urban planning.

summer.description: Hot summers. Visit early morning or evening.

winter.description: Perfect weather for sightseeing and walks.

autumn.description: Pleasant and ideal for outdoor activities.

places.name: Rock Garden
places.description: A 40-acre sculpture garden created by Nek Chand using recycled waste materials. Features over 5,000 sculptures.
places.tips: Wear comfortable shoes — the garden has uneven paths and many sections to explore.

places.name: Sukhna Lake
places.description: A beautiful man-made reservoir at the foothills of the Shivalik Hills, perfect for boating and sunset walks.
places.tips: Visit at sunrise for bird watching or at sunset for stunning views.

places.name: Rose Garden
places.description: Asia's largest rose garden spread over 30 acres, featuring 1,600+ varieties of roses and beautiful landscaping.
places.tips: Best visited during the Rose Festival in February-March when the garden is in full bloom.

places.name: Capitol Complex
places.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site designed by Le Corbusier, housing the High Court, Secretariat, and Legislative Assembly.
places.tips: Guided tours are mandatory. Book in advance through the Chandigarh Tourism website.
